merged_events.event_description: The American Legation in La Paz opened under Chargé d'Affaires John Appleton, who presented his credentials to the Government of the Republic of Bolivia.

evidence.quote: The American Legation in La Paz opened on January 3, 1849, under Chargé d'Affaires John Appleton.

merged_events.event_description: Following a joint announcement by Bolivia and the United States, the American Legation in Bolivia was raised to the rank of Embassy.

evidence.quote: Following a joint announcement by the Government of Bolivia and the Government of the United States on January 4, 1942, the Legation of Bolivia in the United States and the Legation of the United States in Bolivia were both raised to the rank of Embassy.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Legation elevated to Embassy when Pierre de Lagarde Boal presented credentials as the first Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary to Bolivia.

evidence.quote: Pierre De Lagarde Boal, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1942-03-05. Credentials presented: 1942-05-23. Normal relations interrupted on 1943-12-20.

merged_events.event_description: Normal diplomatic relations interrupted following an unrecognized change of government in Bolivia; Boal eventually left post on February 5, 1944.

evidence.quote: Normal relations interrupted on 1943-12-20. Note: The new Government of Bolivia was still unrecognized by the United States when Boal left post on February 5, 1944.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Embassy restored when Walter Clarence Thurston presented credentials as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary.

evidence.quote: Walter Clarence Thurston, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1944-09-21. Credentials presented: 1944-11-16. Left post on 1946-01-03.

merged_events.event_description: Normal diplomatic relations interrupted following an unrecognized change of government in Bolivia; Ambassador Weissman left post on July 20, 1980.

evidence.quote: Normal relations interrupted on 1980-07-17. Note: The new Government of Bolivia had not been recognized by the United States when Weissman left post on July 20, 1980.

merged_events.event_description: Following the interruption of normal relations, Alexander Fletcher Watson served as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim, maintaining an informal U.S. presence in Bolivia.

evidence.quote: Alexander Fletcher Watson, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 1980-07. Left post: 1981-09.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Embassy restored when Edwin Gharst Corr presented credentials as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary.

evidence.quote: Edwin Gharst Corr, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1981-11-25. Credentials presented: 1981-12-10. Left post on 1985-08-09.

matched.notes: Perfect match: both give 1849-01-03 as the date the American Legation in La Paz opened.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Normal diplomatic relations interrupted following an unrecognized change of government in Bolivia. Ambassador Boal eventually left post on 1944-02-05.
missing_from_csv.notes: High confidence from POCOM. This interruption of relations is not reflected in the CSV.

missing_from_csv.event_description: U.S. Embassy restored when Ambassador Walter Clarence Thurston presented credentials.
missing_from_csv.notes: High confidence from POCOM. Restoration of Embassy status after the 1943-12-20 interruption. Date is credential presentation date; no RDCR institutional date available, so POCOM date applies.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Normal diplomatic relations interrupted following an unrecognized change of government in Bolivia. Ambassador Weissman left post on 1980-07-20.
missing_from_csv.notes: High confidence from POCOM. A second interruption of relations not reflected in the CSV.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Following the interruption of normal relations, a Chargé d'Affaires ad interim (Alexander Fletcher Watson) maintained an informal U.S. presence in Bolivia.
missing_from_csv.notes: Low confidence from POCOM. The status as 'Interests' is inferred from the presence of a CDA during a period of non-recognition; the source does not explicitly label this an Interests Section. Treat with caution.

missing_from_csv.event_description: U.S. Embassy restored when Ambassador Edwin Gharst Corr presented credentials.
missing_from_csv.notes: High confidence from POCOM. Restoration of Embassy status after the 1980 interruption. Date is credential presentation date; no RDCR institutional date available, so POCOM date applies.

discrepancies.extracted_value: 1942-01-04 (RDCR: joint announcement raising Legation to Embassy) / 1942-05-23 (POCOM: credential presentation of first Ambassador)
discrepancies.reasoning: RDCR explicitly states that the Legation was raised to Embassy rank following a joint announcement on January 4, 1942. This is an institutional date from the primary source and should take priority. The CSV date of 1942-03-31 does not correspond to any date found in either source — not the RDCR announcement date (Jan 4), the POCOM appointment date (Mar 5), or the POCOM credential presentation date (May 23). The RDCR date of 1942-01-04 is the most authoritative date for this elevation.
